ops.body: Phase-3 live run, second design gap (found live, $0 spent, all pre-container): serve_modal.build_app's serialized=True Function can NEVER deploy from this repo's own runtime — the repo pins Python 3.14t (free-threaded), Modal rejects free-threaded Python for images outright (_image.py _validate_python_version), and serialized=True additionally requires local defining Python == image Python (3.12). The constraint is structurally unsatisfiable from 3.14t; phase-1 review missed it because CI mocks Modal (no live serialized deploy is testable). The spike never hit it: `modal deploy modal_app.py` on a standalone module syncs the source wholesale, no serialization. INTERIM (approved): deploy from a throwaway non-free-threaded 3.12 venv (ops-only; calibration runs from cc-steer-n3 unaffected). DURABLE FIX (decided, lands with phase 4): restructure the app definition to the module-sync shape — self-contained module Modal syncs into the image context — killing serialized=True and both couplings; add a CI-runnable standalone-import check for the app module to replace the untestable serialized constraint. Options (A) image-python bump (trades couplings, shifts substrate) and (C) blessing the throwaway env as procedure were rejected.
